title: Automated dimensional + relationship verification harness (CI gate)
context: Dimensional correctness is the project's core promise: a fraction tile that is 2 mm too narrow, or a number line with unequal intervals, teaches a false mathematical relationship. Checking 'it looks right on screen' is insufficient. The verification harness parses the generated SVG/PDF geometry, asserts every declared dimension against the spec within tolerance, and asserts relational invariants (e.g. 10 unit blocks == 1 rod, two halves == one whole). It must run in CI and block any out-of-tolerance artifact from being released.
objective: Build an automated verification harness that parses generated SVG/PDF geometry, asserts all declared dimensions and relationship invariants within tolerance, checks accessibility properties, and runs as a CI gate that blocks out-of-tolerance releases.
acceptanceCriteria: Parses generated SVG (or PDF) geometry and asserts every declared dimension (width, height, pitch, etc.) from the spec within the style-guide tolerance (+/- 0.5 mm or +/- 0.5%, whichever is larger).
Asserts declared relationship invariants: e.g. sum of N denomination-tiles == one whole tile; equal number-line intervals; 10 unit blocks == 1 rod; verifiable from geometry alone.
Runs in CI (GitHub Actions) and blocks any out-of-tolerance artifact with a non-zero exit code; emits an auditable JSON report (no secrets or PII).
Includes accessibility sub-checks: contrast ratio >= WCAG AA for all text/numerals; detects colour-only encodings; verifies embedded font is on the cleared open-asset list.
Has a test suite with at least one known-good spec (passes) and one known-bad spec (fails); tests pass in pnpm test.
resources: tasks/math-manip-spec-001.json -- spec schema (declares dimensions and invariants to verify)
tasks/math-manip-code-001.json -- generator engine (produces the artifacts to verify)
output: A TypeScript verification package (packages/verifier or similar) in a pull request, MIT-licensed, with a CI workflow file (.github/workflows/verify.yml), a CLI entry point, and test fixtures.
